narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—pouring your heart into law school dreams only to have them paused like this isn't easy, and I'm here to walk you through why our prediction model saw your path to the bar exam as an uphill battle, with an 82% chance of not passing. Picture your application as a story the model read closely: at the top was your LSAT score of 23.5, which stood out as the strongest signal flashing caution, whispering that the analytical sharpness needed for bar success might be a stretch right now. Layered on that was your Year 1 decile of 2, showing early law school grades that placed you low in your class, pulling the narrative firmly toward struggle. Your Year 3 decile of 1 echoed that worry even louder, suggesting those foundational habits haven't shifted enough to build momentum. Coming from a middle-income family added another thread, as the model saw it hinting at fewer resources to buffer the intense demands ahead. Your Asian background and undergraduate GPA of 2.6 wove in subtle risks too, patterns from past students where these aligned with tougher bar outcomes. Even being full-time and female, while strengths in many stories, tipped slightly toward failure in this model's read of the full picture. It's not that any one piece defined you—it's how they combined into a portrait of someone who, based on similar journeys, faces steep odds without big changes. This isn't the end of your story; it's a nudge to boost that LSAT, climb those deciles, and reshape what comes next. You've got resilience—use this to pivot stronger. With care, Your Admissions Guide
